payload.text:
You are a senior full-stack UI engineer + solution architect. Build a modern, clean, efficient single-page web app (React) with an excellent UX (not old-school). Use a premium SaaS look and feel: spacing, cards, subtle shadows, responsive layout, clear typography, and great loading/empty/error states. Keep it simple but polished.

APP NAME
- “Invoice → X12 810 EDI + Barcode Sheet”

PRIMARY USER GOAL
- User uploads an invoice photo (JPG/PNG).
- On Submit, app ALWAYS generates:
  (1) X12 810 EDI text (North America invoices)
- Additionally, ONLY IF a barcode/QR/UCI is detected in the image, app generates:
  (2) A barcode label sheet PDF (grid of labels) with each detected code rendered as a barcode and the value printed underneath.

IMPORTANT DESIGN REQUIREMENTS (UI)
- Landing page with:
  - Header: title + short subtitle
  - Left panel: Upload card (drag & drop + click to browse)
  - Right panel: Results card (tabs: “EDI Preview” and “Barcode Sheet”)
- Show uploaded image preview (thumbnail) + file name + reset/replace button.
- Show a clear “Submit” CTA.
- Use a step progress indicator during processing:
  1) “Scanning barcode locally…”
  2) “Understanding invoice…”
  3) “Generating EDI…”
  4) “Generating barcode PDF…” (only when needed)
- After completion:
  - EDI tab shows formatted monospaced text area (read-only) with copy button + download .edi button.
  - Barcode tab:
    - If codes exist: show a compact list of detected codes (type + value) and a PDF preview embed (or a generated label grid preview image) plus download PDF button.
    - If no codes: show a friendly empty state “No barcode/QR/UCI detected. Barcode sheet not generated.”
- Include a “Quality / Validation” panel that lists:
  - required fields missing for EDI (if any)
  - warnings (e.g., totals mismatch)
  - an overall status badge: “Ready” vs “Needs Review”
- The UI must stay responsive and not freeze.

TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS (NO MESS)
- Barcode/QR detection MUST be done locally in the browser using a barcode decoding library (not the model):
  - Prefer @zxing/browser (ZXing) for QR + common 1D barcodes.
  - If needed, add quagga2 as fallback for UPC/EAN.
- The model (Gemini) focuses on invoice understanding + generating EDI from extracted facts.
- Do NOT hardcode API keys. Use AI Studio environment/secure config if available.
- Never “guess” missing invoice values. If a required value is missing, leave it null/empty and mark “Needs Review”.

DATA FLOW (MUST FOLLOW)
1) User uploads invoice image.
2) On Submit:
   A) Barcode scan step (local):
      - Extract detected_codes = [{type, value}] from the image.
      - Also detect UCI patterns in decoded strings if applicable (treat as code type “UCI” when value matches UCI heuristics).
   B) Invoice understanding step (Gemini multimodal):
      - Send the image to Gemini with strict instructions to extract normalized invoice fields into JSON.
      - Then generate X12 810 EDI text using those extracted facts ONLY.
3) Output:
   - Always produce edi_810 string.
   - Produce barcode_pdf only if detected_codes length > 0.
4) Show preview + provide downloads.

STRICT JSON CONTRACT FROM GEMINI (ABSOLUTE)
Gemini must return VALID JSON ONLY, no markdown, no commentary:
{
  "invoice": {
    "invoice_number": "",
    "invoice_date": "",
    "seller": {"name":"", "address":"", "tax_id":""},
    "buyer": {"name":"", "address":"", "tax_id":""},
    "ship_to": {"name":"", "address":""},
    "po_number": "",
    "currency": "USD",
    "payment_terms": "",
    "totals": {"subtotal": null, "tax": null, "total": null},
    "line_items": [
      {"description":"", "sku":"", "qty": null, "uom":"", "unit_price": null, "line_total": null}
    ]
  },
  "edi_810": "",
  "missing_fields": [],
  "warnings": [],
  "needs_review": false
}

EDI REQUIREMENTS (X12 810, NORTH AMERICA)
- Generate an X12 810 INVOICE.
- Use conservative, common-sense defaults ONLY when they are structural (like segment separators), but never fabricate invoice facts.
- Include at minimum: ST, BIG, N1 loops for seller/buyer when available, IT1 loop for line items when available, TDS if total is available, TXI if tax is available, and SE.
- If envelope segments (ISA/GS/GE/IEA) are not confidently derivable, then OMIT envelopes and generate transaction set only (ST..SE). Do not invent control numbers.
- If critical fields are missing (invoice number or invoice date or total), still generate EDI but set needs_review=true and list missing_fields; use empty values where unknown (don’t hallucinate).

VALIDATION RULES (NO ROOM FOR MISTAKES)
- If invoice_number missing => needs_review true.
- If invoice_date missing => needs_review true.
- If totals.total missing => needs_review true.
- If line items missing completely, still generate header-level EDI; add warning “No line items detected”.
- If subtotal + tax != total (when all present), add warning “Totals mismatch”.
- Display missing_fields and warnings in UI.

BARCODE PDF REQUIREMENTS
- Only generate when detected_codes length > 0.
- Generate a label sheet PDF with a neat grid (e.g., 3 columns).
- Each label shows:
  - code type (small)
  - barcode graphic
  - human readable value under it
- Use jsPDF to create the PDF.
- Generate barcode graphics using a browser-friendly library:
  - Use bwip-js (browser build) OR JsBarcode to draw barcode onto canvas/SVG then place into PDF.
- Support QR codes as QR graphics if type is QR.
- Ensure it looks clean and similar to retail label stickers (like price tags), but without needing price if not available.

UI COMPONENTS & STYLE
- Use TailwindCSS for styling if available in this environment.
- Use a modern component look (cards, buttons, tabs, badges, toasts).
- Make it accessible: keyboard focus states, ARIA labels for upload and buttons.

ERROR HANDLING
- If barcode scan fails, show warning but proceed with EDI generation.
- If Gemini call fails, show error with retry button.
- Never crash; always show a clear state.

DELIVERABLE
- Generate the full working app code in AI Studio Build.
- Include all dependencies and ensure it runs in the preview.
- Provide sensible defaults and a clean, professional UX.

NOW BUILD IT.

**Initiating Image Processing**

I'm now focused on the initial image processing for the React SPA. My primary goal is to incorporate the ability to upload JPG and PNG invoice images. From there, I'll be implementing the first step: local barcode detection using `@zxing/library`. This involves scanning the uploaded image to identify any barcodes, QR codes, or UCIs present within it.


**Defining Next Steps**

I've outlined the subsequent stages: leveraging Gemini for data extraction and X12 810 EDI generation from detected invoice images. Following this, I'll validate essential fields and provide previews of both the EDI and barcode sheet PDFs. Finally, I'll allow downloads of the `.edi` and `.pdf` files.

Tabs for EDI preview and Barcode Sheet results.
Visual indicator of processing stages.
Status badge component showing Ready or Needs Review status.
Local barcode scanning service using ZXing.
Integration with Gemini for invoice understanding and EDI generation.
Generates label sheet PDF with barcodes using jsPDF and a barcode library.

payload.text:
Act as a senior product UI engineer + EDI integration architect. Build a modern single-page React web app with a polished SaaS UI (clean cards, great spacing, responsive layout, progress steps, tabs, badges, toasts). Not old-school.

APP NAME
Invoice → X12 810 + Barcode Sheet (Prototype)

USER FLOW
1) User uploads an invoice photo (JPG/PNG).
2) User clicks Submit.
3) App ALWAYS generates:
   - X12 810 EDI text (North America)
4) App generates Barcode Sheet PDF ONLY IF UPC/QR/UCI values exist.
5) App shows preview of EDI and preview of barcode PDF, and offers download buttons.

CRITICAL FIX: DO NOT TRY TO DECODE THE BLACK BARS FROM THE IMAGE
This invoice contains many tiny barcodes. Barcode decoding from a still photo often fails.
Instead, extract codes from printed text:
- Use Tesseract.js OCR in the browser to read the image into text.
- From OCR text, extract UPC codes via regex:
  - UPC-A: 12 digits
  - EAN-13: 13 digits
- Also extract UCI numbers if present using:
  - any “UCI” label followed by alphanumerics OR any 10–20 digit id near “UCI”
- Deduplicate codes. Treat these as detected_codes.

UI REQUIREMENTS
Landing layout:
- Header with title + short subtitle.
- Left panel: Upload card (drag/drop + browse) + image preview + Replace/Reset.
- Right panel: Results card with tabs:
  - Tab 1: “EDI Preview”
  - Tab 2: “Barcode Sheet”
- Show step progress during submit:
  1) “Running OCR to find UPC/UCI…”
  2) “Understanding invoice…”
  3) “Generating EDI…”
  4) “Generating barcode PDF…” (only if codes exist)

EDI PREVIEW
- Show formatted EDI for humans:
  - render segments on new lines by replacing "~" with "~\n"
- Provide:
  - Copy button
  - Download .edi button (download raw EDI string)

BARCODE SHEET TAB
- If detected_codes not empty:
  - Show a compact list of codes (type + value)
  - Render a PDF preview (embed) and allow download
- If empty:
  - Friendly empty state “No UPC/QR/UCI detected. Barcode sheet not generated.”

QUALITY PANEL
Show:
- missing_fields
- warnings
- status badge:
  - “Ready” when needs_review=false
  - “Needs Review” when needs_review=true

TECH / DEPENDENCIES
- OCR: tesseract.js (browser)
- Barcode rendering:
  - Use JsBarcode for 1D codes (UPC/EAN/Code128 as fallback)
  - Use a QR generator for QR codes
- PDF generation: jsPDF
- Styling: TailwindCSS (if available)
- Accessibility: ARIA labels, focus states.

MODEL USAGE (GEMINI)
Gemini should NOT be responsible for barcode decoding.
Gemini is used for invoice understanding + EDI creation from facts only.

INPUTS TO GEMINI
Send:
- The invoice image
- The OCR text result
- The extracted detected_codes list (UPC/UCI)

STRICT OUTPUT: JSON ONLY (NO MARKDOWN, NO EXTRA TEXT)
Return:
{
  "invoice": {
    "invoice_number": "",
    "invoice_date": "",
    "seller": {"name":"", "address":"", "city":"", "state":"", "zip":"", "tax_id":""},
    "buyer": {"name":"", "address":"", "city":"", "state":"", "zip":"", "tax_id":""},
    "po_number": "",
    "currency": "USD",
    "totals": {"subtotal": null, "tax": null, "total": null, "discount": null},
    "line_items": [
      {"line_no": 1, "description":"", "qty": null, "uom":"", "unit_price": null, "line_total": null, "upc":""}
    ]
  },
  "edi_810": "",
  "missing_fields": [],
  "warnings": [],
  "needs_review": false,
  "detected_codes": []
}

NON-HALLUCINATION RULES (NO ROOM FOR MISTAKES)
- Use ONLY what is visible in image or OCR text.
- If not found, leave blank/null and add to missing_fields.
- NEVER invent totals, addresses, invoice number, dates, PO number.

INVOICE PARTY MAPPING RULES (IMPORTANT)
- “Seller/Supplier” is the company printed at the top header of invoice/receipt (example: MAPLE CITY ICE CO.)
- “Buyer” is the customer/account block (example: GANESH INC).
- Do not swap these roles.

EDI GENERATION RULES (X12 810)
- Generate ST..SE only (no ISA/GS envelopes).
- Minimum segments:
  ST*810*0001~
  BIG*<invoice_date_yyyymmdd>*<invoice_number>~
  N1*SU*<seller_name>~
  N3*<seller_address_if_known>~
  N4*<seller_city>*<seller_state>*<seller_zip>~
  N1*BY*<buyer_name>~
  N3*<buyer_address>~
  N4*<buyer_city>*<buyer_state>*<buyer_zip>~
  IT1 loops for each line item:
    IT1*<line_no>*<qty>*<uom>*<unit_price>**UP*<upc>~
    PID*F****<description>~
  TDS*<total_cents>~  (only if total exists)
  CTT*<line_item_count>~
  SE*<segment_count>*0001~
- If invoice_number/date/total missing => needs_review=true and list missing_fields.
- If any UPC extracted is clearly invalid (not 12/13 digits) => warning.
- If OCR shows many line items but model captured too few => warning “Possible truncation: review line items”.

BARCODE PDF GENERATION RULES
- Only generate if detected_codes.length > 0.
- Create a clean label-sheet PDF grid (3 columns).
- For each code:
  - Show code type small text
  - Render barcode:
    - If 12-digit => UPC format if supported; else Code128
    - If 13-digit => EAN13 if supported; else Code128
    - If QR => render QR
  - Print human readable value underneath
- Save as a blob and show preview + download.

ERROR HANDLING
- If OCR fails => show warning and still call Gemini with the image; detected_codes becomes [].
- If Gemini fails => show error + Retry.

DELIVERABLE
Generate the full working app in AI Studio Build, including dependencies, with a professional UI and all logic above.
Build now.

**Refining Application Architecture**

I'm now prioritizing the new approach. The React SPA will be updated to handle invoice image conversion to X12 810 EDI, plus barcode sheet PDF generation. The existing barcode scanning from local images has proven unreliable, so it is being replaced. This new system must handle image upload and processing efficiently.


**Developing Feature Components**

I've been mapping out the technical components needed. OCR will come from Tesseract.js in the browser, extracting barcode data with regex. Gemini will handle invoice understanding and EDI generation. I plan to use JsBarcode and a QR code generator for PDF barcodes, and jspdf for PDF creation. I'll implement lucide-react for icons, @google/genai for Gemini, and Tailwind CSS for styling. I've also outlined the UI layout with upload/preview on the left and results with tabs on the right, plus a quality panel. Finally, I'm focusing on the ST..SE segment and the segments within those to render the EDI document.


**Implementing Core Logic**

I'm now focused on the implementation details. I'm updating the `importmap` in `index.html` for Tesseract.js and JsBarcode. I'm also revising type definitions in `types.ts`, and building out the service layer. New services include `ocrService.ts`, `barcodeExtractor.ts`, `geminiService.ts`, and `pdfGenerator.ts`. I'll orchestrate the flow in `App.tsx` and refine the results panel UI. I am considering UCI formats, and Gemini-3-flash-preview.

entries.description: Update import map to include Tesseract.js, JsBarcode, and QRCode libraries for OCR and barcode generation.
Refine types for broader invoice data and OCR processing steps.
New service for browser-based OCR and regex-based code extraction.
Update Gemini service to accept OCR text and detected codes, ensuring high accuracy and no hallucinations.
Redesigned PDF generator using JsBarcode and QRCode for accurate label rendering.
Integrate new OCR-based flow into the main application logic.
Polished results display with human-readable EDI and PDF preview capabilities.

diagnostics.message: Property 'SCANNING_BARCODES' does not exist on type 'typeof ProcessingStep'.
Type '"UCI / Serial"' is not assignable to type '"UPC-A" | "EAN-13" | "UCI" | "QR" | "CODE128"'.

payload.text:
**Addressing React Errors**

I'm currently focused on rectifying two errors in the React application. The initial task involves resolving the "Property 'SCANNING_BARCODES' does not exist" error on line 13 of `components/StepProgress.tsx`. My investigation points to the need to examine `types.ts` to identify and integrate the missing property definition.


**Refining Enum and Type**

I've been examining the `ProcessingStep` enum and the `DetectedCode` type. It appears the `StepProgress` component mistakenly references `SCANNING_BARCODES`, but the enum uses `RUNNING_OCR`. I'll update the component. Moreover, there's a type mismatch in `barcodeScanner.ts`. I found an incorrect assignment of `"UCI / Serial"`, which is not permitted. I'll change it to `"UCI"` since it is a valid type, as it already exists within the allowed values for `DetectedCode['type']`.


**Updating Type References**

I've addressed the type mismatches in `barcodeScanner.ts`. Specifically, I corrected the erroneous assignment of `"UCI / Serial"` to simply `"UCI"`. This change ensures the code aligns with the permitted values within the `DetectedCode['type']` type definition. Also, I updated `StepProgress.tsx`, changing the incorrectly referenced step from `SCANNING_BARCODES` to `RUNNING_OCR` to match the expected state progression.


**Reviewing Workflow Steps**

I'm now focusing on the flow within `App.tsx` and how it maps to `ProcessingStep`. It seems `GENERATING_EDI` is bundled with `UNDERSTANDING_INVOICE`, which isn't a problem but worth noting. Additionally, I revisited `StepProgress.tsx` to confirm that the enum keys are properly referenced. I've corrected line 13 to correctly reference `RUNNING_OCR`, aligning it with the enum. I also took the time to verify the `ProcessingStep` enum definition within `types.ts` again, to confirm that it's as expected.


**Confirming State and References**

I've verified the state progression and ensured correct references within `StepProgress.tsx`. Furthermore, I've confirmed `barcodeScanner.ts` uses the correct `DetectedCode` type value. Now, I'm verifying the enum values used within the application. I also need to verify that `geminiService.ts` is using the specified model, and that the model is being passed correctly into `ai.models.generateContent`. I've made sure to use `gemini-3-flash-preview`.


**Validating Gemini Model Usage**

I've been reviewing the model selection for data extraction and text processing. After considering the nature of the task, I've confirmed that using `gemini-3-flash-preview` is the appropriate choice, as it's categorized under basic text tasks. Further review of `geminiService.ts` confirms the correct model is being invoked: `gemini-3-flash-preview` is correctly specified and passed to the `generateContent` method.


**Confirming Data Structures**

Now, I'm verifying the data structures used for the Gemini API responses to ensure the extracted data is handled correctly. I noticed the responses are parsed as JSON, but there could be errors. I've confirmed that the JSON parsing is in a `try...catch` block. I'll need to confirm what the error states when an error is caught.


**Verifying Error Handling**

I'm now focusing on error handling in the Gemini API response parsing. Specifically, I'm verifying the `try...catch` block around the JSON parsing of the response from `geminiService.ts`. I need to confirm the error message handling, especially since the parsing has failed. I also need to ensure that the error states, like "Failed to parse Gemini response as valid JSON", are accounted for. Next, I'll revisit `App.tsx` and `types.ts` to double-check step name references. I noticed that the `StepProgress.tsx` component is referencing a nonexistent `SCANNING_BARCODES` constant and I'll need to confirm that this is correctly mapped in the states.


**Correcting Step Logic**

I'm now investigating how the `StepProgress` component uses the `ProcessingStep` enum. It seems the `steps` array uses enum *values* as IDs, while `getStepStatus` expects enum *keys*. This causes incorrect indexing. I'll need to modify the array or the logic to ensure the correct step progression is displayed. Also, I'm checking that all steps are displayed correctly using the current implementation, and that the appropriate progress is shown.


**Confirming Step Identification**

I'm now verifying the current implementation within `StepProgress.tsx` to align the `ProcessingStep` enum as type for `stepId`, which is passed from the `App.tsx` component. The `step.id` variable resolves correctly and is equal to the enum value, e.g. `ProcessingStep.RUNNING_OCR`. I also reviewed `services/barcodeScanner.ts`, which needs to be updated. Line 47 needs to be changed from `'UCI / Serial'` to `'UCI'`. I'm now generating the XML changes.


**Adjusting Step Progression**

payload.text:
You are a senior full-stack UI engineer (20+ years) and an EDI integration architect. Build a premium multi-step React web app in Google AI Studio Build. The UI must be modern and clean (SaaS-like), not cluttered, with excellent UX, responsive layout, accessible controls, and strong empty/loading/error states.

APP NAME
Invoice Batch Processor → X12 810 EDI + Barcode Label Sheets

CORE GOAL
User uploads 1–4 invoice images at once. The app:
- Classifies invoices by Seller (supplier) automatically.
- Always generates X12 810 EDI for each invoice.
- Generates barcode label sheet PDF per invoice ONLY if UPC/EAN/UCI/QR codes exist and are validated.
- Provides a final Results experience with previews + downloads.
- Includes an Edit workflow that updates EDI + barcode PDF after user corrections.

IMPORTANT: PROTOTYPE ONLY (AI Studio Build)
No production auth, no backend required. Keep everything in-browser plus Gemini call(s). Do not require Cloud Storage/Run.

MULTI-PAGE / MULTI-STEP UX (MUST)
Implement a 3-step wizard with routing-like navigation:
PAGE 1 — Upload
- Header + short description
- Multi-upload (drag & drop) accepting 1–4 images (JPG/PNG)
- Show image thumbnails in a grid, each with filename, remove button, and reorder (optional).
- Button: “Generate”
- Input: “Batch Name” optional
- Optional toggle: “Strict Mode (recommended)” ON by default

PAGE 2 — Processing & Classification
- Stepper/progress view showing batch processing status per invoice:
  1) Preprocess image
  2) OCR full page
  3) OCR UPC column
  4) Extract & validate codes
  5) Understand invoice (Gemini)
  6) Build EDI
  7) Build Barcode PDF (if codes valid)
- Show a live list of invoices with status chips (Queued / Running / Done / Needs Review / Error).
- Show detected Seller name per invoice as soon as known.
- When finished, show summary:
  - Total invoices processed
  - Sellers found (group counts)
  - Total codes detected/valid/invalid
- Buttons: “Back to Upload” and “View Results”

PAGE 3 — Results (Grouped by Seller)
- Left sidebar:
  - Seller filters (each seller with count)
  - “All invoices” option
- Main content:
  - For each invoice: a collapsible card (accordion)
    - Header shows: invoice number/date (if found), seller, buyer, totals, status badge Ready/Needs Review
    - Tabs inside each invoice card:
      A) EDI Preview
      B) Barcode Sheet
      C) Quality & Data (with Edit)
- Global actions:
  - “Download All EDI (zip-like multiple downloads)” (if zip not possible, download sequentially)
  - “Download All Barcode PDFs” (only where available)
  - “Export Summary JSON” (for debugging)

EDIT WORKFLOW (MUST)
In “Quality & Data” tab:
- Show a Review Table for line items (editable):
  columns: line_no, description, qty, uom, unit_price, line_total, code_type, code_value, code_status
- Highlight invalid or suspicious codes.
- Allow user to edit values inline (especially code_value, qty, unit_price, description, uom).
- Provide buttons:
  - “Re-validate Codes”
  - “Regenerate EDI & Barcode PDF”
  - “Cancel edits”
- When user saves/regenerates:
  - Update EDI preview, downloadable EDI, barcode PDF preview and downloadable PDF
- Strict Mode behavior:
  - In Strict Mode, invalid codes MUST NOT render as barcodes and MUST NOT be inserted into IT1 UP product id.
  - Show “INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED” tiles on barcode sheet until fixed.

BARCODE / CODE EXTRACTION (CRITICAL)
DO NOT attempt to decode the black bars from the image. Many tiny barcodes in a photo cause failures.
Instead:
1) Preprocess the image for OCR in browser:
   - grayscale, contrast boost, sharpen, upscale 2x
2) Run Tesseract.js OCR on full image to get general text.
3) Run a second OCR pass on the UPC column region:
   - Find “UPC” header location if possible from OCR
   - Else use a heuristic crop around the UPC column x-range (right-middle column in receipt layout)
4) Extract candidate codes from OCR text:
   - UPC-A: 11–12 digits
   - EAN-13: 13 digits
   - UCI: any token near “UCI” label or 10–20 digit/alpha numeric IDs with UCI context
   - QR: if OCR reveals a QR payload string (optional)
5) Normalize:
   - remove spaces/punctuation
   - preserve leading zeros (codes are strings)
6) Deduplicate codes.

VALIDATION & REPAIR (MUST, TO FIX BAD PREVIEW)
Implement strict UPC/EAN validation:
- UPC-A 12 digits: check digit validation
- EAN-13 13 digits: check digit validation
- If 11 digits:
  - Try prepend “0” => 12 digits, validate
  - If still invalid and Strict Mode OFF (testing only), allow computing check digit to form 12-digit UPC.
- If still invalid:
  - mark invalid and needs_review=true
  - NEVER render UPC/EAN barcode for invalid codes in Strict Mode
  - NEVER output placeholder codes like 000000000000
Add warnings for:
- wrong length
- failed check digit
- suspicious leading zeros beyond normal UPC patterns
- OCR mismatch between UPC column and line item link

BARCODE PDF GENERATION (MUST NEVER SHOW “[BARCODE ERROR]”)
Observed issue: some labels show “[BARCODE ERROR]” due to invalid/incorrect codes.
Fix by design:
- Wrap barcode rendering in try/catch.
- On any render error: do NOT show error text; show a clean “INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED” tile.
- Strict Mode default ON: no fallback barcodes for invalid codes.
- Optional toggle: “Allow Code128 fallback (testing only)”
  - If enabled and code invalid, render Code128 with big “FALLBACK” watermark and keep needs_review=true.

Barcode rendering implementation:
- Use jsPDF for PDF.
- Use bwip-js OR JsBarcode to render UPC-A/EAN-13 (prefer robust library).
- Use a QR library for QR.
- Layout: 3 columns grid, clean label design:
  - Optional price line if available
  - description line (truncate)
  - barcode (or invalid tile)
  - human readable code under it

MULTI-INVOICE CLASSIFICATION (SELLER GROUPING)
Use OCR + Gemini to determine seller name for each invoice:
- Seller is the company name printed at the top header of the invoice/receipt.
- Buyer is the customer/account block.
On Page 3, group invoice cards under seller filters.

GEMINI USAGE (INVOICE UNDERSTANDING + EDI ONLY)
Gemini must not do barcode decoding.
Gemini input per invoice:
- the invoice image
- OCR text (full + UPC column OCR)
- preliminary extracted line items (best-effort) and validated code list

STRICT OUTPUT FROM GEMINI: JSON ONLY (NO MARKDOWN)
Return:
{
  "invoice": {
    "invoice_number": "",
    "invoice_date": "",
    "seller": {"name":"", "address":"", "city":"", "state":"", "zip":""},
    "buyer": {"name":"", "address":"", "city":"", "state":"", "zip":"", "account_id":""},
    "po_number": "",
    "currency": "USD",
    "totals": {"subtotal": null, "tax": null, "discount": null, "total": null},
    "line_items": [
      {"line_no": 1, "description":"", "qty": null, "uom":"CA", "unit_price": null, "line_total": null, "code_type":"UPC", "code_value":""}
    ]
  },
  "missing_fields": [],
  "warnings": [],
  "needs_review": false
}

NON-HALLUCINATION RULES (NO ROOM FOR MISTAKES)
- Use ONLY values present in image/OCR.
- If a field is uncertain, leave blank/null and add it to missing_fields.
- Do not invent totals, dates, invoice numbers, UPCs, addresses.

EDI GENERATION (X12 810, ST..SE ONLY)
Generate EDI in-app deterministically from the reviewed invoice JSON (not free-form).
Rules:
- Produce ST..SE only. No ISA/GS envelopes.
- BIG formatting must be correct and consistent:
  BIG*YYYYMMDD*INVOICENUM***~
- Party mapping:
  - N1*SU = seller header company
  - N1*BY = buyer/customer
- Add N3/N4 only if address parts exist.
- IT1 loop per line item:
  IT1*<line_no>*<qty>*<uom>*<unit_price>**UP*<code>~
  - Include UP product id ONLY if code validated (Strict Mode).
  - If code invalid: omit UP element and add warning; needs_review true.
  PID*F****<description>~
- Totals:
  - TDS*<total_cents>~ only if total exists and is numeric
- CTT with line count
- SE segment count must be correct.

HUMAN-FRIENDLY EDI PREVIEW
- Display EDI with each segment on its own line (render "~\n").
- Download should be raw EDI string with "~" terminators.

DOWNLOADS
- Each invoice card should have:
  - Download EDI
  - Download Barcode PDF (if generated)
- Batch downloads:
  - Download all EDI sequentially
  - Download all barcode PDFs sequentially

STATE MANAGEMENT
Maintain per-invoice state:
- file, preview url
- ocr_full_text, ocr_upc_text
- detected_codes_raw, detected_codes_valid, detected_codes_invalid
- invoice_json (from Gemini)
- user_edits (line items edits)
- needs_review, missing_fields, warnings
- edi_raw, edi_preview
- barcode_pdf_blob_url (optional)
Also maintain batch-level summary and seller groups.

STYLING / COMPONENTS
Use TailwindCSS if available.
Use clean components: cards, stepper, tabs, badge, accordion, table with sticky header, toast.
Make it accessible with keyboard and ARIA labels.

ERROR HANDLING
- OCR fails: show warning and still attempt Gemini with the image.
- Gemini fails: show per-invoice error + Retry.
- Barcode rendering fails: never show “[BARCODE ERROR]”; show “INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED” tile.

DELIVERABLE
Generate the full working app code in AI Studio Build with the 3-page UX, multi-upload, seller grouping, edit/regenerate loop, strict validation, correct X12 810 formatting, and robust barcode PDF generation that never outputs barcode errors.
Build now.

**Designing the React Application**

I'm now focusing on the user experience. Page 1 will handle multi-file uploads with a grid layout. The goal is a straightforward, visually intuitive process for the user to select and prepare the invoice images for processing. I'm prioritizing ease of use for the upload stage.


**Defining the EDI Generation**

I've progressed to defining the EDI generation. This is deterministic, ensuring predictable outputs. I am still iterating on a robust barcode extraction and validation approach using Tesseract.js OCR, regex, and strict validation checks to support different barcode formats and the "Strict Mode". Finally, generating barcode PDFs with `jsPDF` and supporting libraries is in progress, including handling of invalid codes.


**Outlining the Project Structure**

I'm solidifying the project's structure, focusing on a clear component and service breakdown. I'm defining interfaces in `types.ts` for all state, results, and steps, and have started laying out service modules. I'm focusing on defining services for OCR, Gemini, EDI, and PDF generation, and components for upload, processing, and results display. I'm setting up a navigation state machine using `App.tsx`.

Implement strict UPC/EAN check-digit validation logic.
Implement deterministic X12 810 EDI generation from validated invoice JSON data.
Implement robust barcode label sheet generation with retail styling and error fallback tiles.
Refine Gemini invoice extraction with strict multi-modal input and JSON validation.
Implement core wizard-based application state and multi-step workflow logic.

You are a senior full-stack UI engineer (20+ years) and an EDI integration architect. Build a premium multi-step React web app in Google AI Studio Build. The UI must be modern and clean (SaaS-like), not cluttered, with excellent UX, responsive layout, accessible controls, and strong empty/loading/error states.

APP NAME
Invoice Batch Processor → X12 810 EDI + Barcode Label Sheets

CORE GOAL
User uploads 1–4 invoice images at once. The app:
- Classifies invoices by Seller (supplier) automatically.
- Always generates X12 810 EDI for each invoice.
- Generates barcode label sheet PDF per invoice ONLY if UPC/EAN/UCI/QR codes exist and are validated.
- Provides a final Results experience with previews + downloads.
- Includes an Edit workflow that updates EDI + barcode PDF after user corrections.

IMPORTANT: PROTOTYPE ONLY (AI Studio Build)
No production auth, no backend required. Keep everything in-browser plus Gemini call(s). Do not require Cloud Storage/Run.

MULTI-PAGE / MULTI-STEP UX (MUST)
Implement a 3-step wizard with routing-like navigation:
PAGE 1 — Upload
- Header + short description
- Multi-upload (drag & drop) accepting 1–4 images (JPG/PNG)
- Show image thumbnails in a grid, each with filename, remove button, and reorder (optional).
- Button: “Generate”
- Input: “Batch Name” optional
- Optional toggle: “Strict Mode (recommended)” ON by default

PAGE 2 — Processing & Classification
- Stepper/progress view showing batch processing status per invoice:
  1) Preprocess image
  2) OCR full page
  3) OCR UPC column
  4) Extract & validate codes
  5) Understand invoice (Gemini)
  6) Build EDI
  7) Build Barcode PDF (if codes valid)
- Show a live list of invoices with status chips (Queued / Running / Done / Needs Review / Error).
- Show detected Seller name per invoice as soon as known.
- When finished, show summary:
  - Total invoices processed
  - Sellers found (group counts)
  - Total codes detected/valid/invalid
- Buttons: “Back to Upload” and “View Results”

PAGE 3 — Results (Grouped by Seller)
- Left sidebar:
  - Seller filters (each seller with count)
  - “All invoices” option
- Main content:
  - For each invoice: a collapsible card (accordion)
    - Header shows: invoice number/date (if found), seller, buyer, totals, status badge Ready/Needs Review
    - Tabs inside each invoice card:
      A) EDI Preview
      B) Barcode Sheet
      C) Quality & Data (with Edit)
- Global actions:
  - “Download All EDI (zip-like multiple downloads)” (if zip not possible, download sequentially)
  - “Download All Barcode PDFs” (only where available)
  - “Export Summary JSON” (for debugging)

EDIT WORKFLOW (MUST)
In “Quality & Data” tab:
- Show a Review Table for line items (editable):
  columns: line_no, description, qty, uom, unit_price, line_total, code_type, code_value, code_status
- Highlight invalid or suspicious codes.
- Allow user to edit values inline (especially code_value, qty, unit_price, description, uom).
- Provide buttons:
  - “Re-validate Codes”
  - “Regenerate EDI & Barcode PDF”
  - “Cancel edits”
- When user saves/regenerates:
  - Update EDI preview, downloadable EDI, barcode PDF preview and downloadable PDF
- Strict Mode behavior:
  - In Strict Mode, invalid codes MUST NOT render as barcodes and MUST NOT be inserted into IT1 UP product id.
  - Show “INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED” tiles on barcode sheet until fixed.

BARCODE / CODE EXTRACTION (CRITICAL)
DO NOT attempt to decode the black bars from the image. Many tiny barcodes in a photo cause failures.
Instead:
1) Preprocess the image for OCR in browser:
   - grayscale, contrast boost, sharpen, upscale 2x
2) Run Tesseract.js OCR on full image to get general text.
3) Run a second OCR pass on the UPC column region:
   - Find “UPC” header location if possible from OCR
   - Else use a heuristic crop around the UPC column x-range (right-middle column in receipt layout)
4) Extract candidate codes from OCR text:
   - UPC-A: 11–12 digits
   - EAN-13: 13 digits
   - UCI: any token near “UCI” label or 10–20 digit/alpha numeric IDs with UCI context
   - QR: if OCR reveals a QR payload string (optional)
5) Normalize:
   - remove spaces/punctuation
   - preserve leading zeros (codes are strings)
6) Deduplicate codes.

VALIDATION & REPAIR (MUST, TO FIX BAD PREVIEW)
Implement strict UPC/EAN validation:
- UPC-A 12 digits: check digit validation
- EAN-13 13 digits: check digit validation
- If 11 digits:
  - Try prepend “0” => 12 digits, validate
  - If still invalid and Strict Mode OFF (testing only), allow computing check digit to form 12-digit UPC.
- If still invalid:
  - mark invalid and needs_review=true
  - NEVER render UPC/EAN barcode for invalid codes in Strict Mode
  - NEVER output placeholder codes like 000000000000
Add warnings for:
- wrong length
- failed check digit
- suspicious leading zeros beyond normal UPC patterns
- OCR mismatch between UPC column and line item link

BARCODE PDF GENERATION (MUST NEVER SHOW “[BARCODE ERROR]”)
Observed issue: some labels show “[BARCODE ERROR]” due to invalid/incorrect codes.
Fix by design:
- Wrap barcode rendering in try/catch.
- On any render error: do NOT show error text; show a clean “INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED” tile.
- Strict Mode default ON: no fallback barcodes for invalid codes.
- Optional toggle: “Allow Code128 fallback (testing only)”
  - If enabled and code invalid, render Code128 with big “FALLBACK” watermark and keep needs_review=true.

Barcode rendering implementation:
- Use jsPDF for PDF.
- Use bwip-js OR JsBarcode to render UPC-A/EAN-13 (prefer robust library).
- Use a QR library for QR.
- Layout: 3 columns grid, clean label design:
  - Optional price line if available
  - description line (truncate)
  - barcode (or invalid tile)
  - human readable code under it

MULTI-INVOICE CLASSIFICATION (SELLER GROUPING)
Use OCR + Gemini to determine seller name for each invoice:
- Seller is the company name printed at the top header of the invoice/receipt.
- Buyer is the customer/account block.
On Page 3, group invoice cards under seller filters.

GEMINI USAGE (INVOICE UNDERSTANDING + EDI ONLY)
Gemini must not do barcode decoding.
Gemini input per invoice:
- the invoice image
- OCR text (full + UPC column OCR)
- preliminary extracted line items (best-effort) and validated code list

STRICT OUTPUT FROM GEMINI: JSON ONLY (NO MARKDOWN)
Return:
{
  "invoice": {
    "invoice_number": "",
    "invoice_date": "",
    "seller": {"name":"", "address":"", "city":"", "state":"", "zip":""},
    "buyer": {"name":"", "address":"", "city":"", "state":"", "zip":"", "account_id":""},
    "po_number": "",
    "currency": "USD",
    "totals": {"subtotal": null, "tax": null, "discount": null, "total": null},
    "line_items": [
      {"line_no": 1, "description":"", "qty": null, "uom":"CA", "unit_price": null, "line_total": null, "code_type":"UPC", "code_value":""}
    ]
  },
  "missing_fields": [],
  "warnings": [],
  "needs_review": false
}

NON-HALLUCINATION RULES (NO ROOM FOR MISTAKES)
- Use ONLY values present in image/OCR.
- If a field is uncertain, leave blank/null and add it to missing_fields.
- Do not invent totals, dates, invoice numbers, UPCs, addresses.

EDI GENERATION (X12 810, ST..SE ONLY)
Generate EDI in-app deterministically from the reviewed invoice JSON (not free-form).
Rules:
- Produce ST..SE only. No ISA/GS envelopes.
- BIG formatting must be correct and consistent:
  BIG*YYYYMMDD*INVOICENUM***~
- Party mapping:
  - N1*SU = seller header company
  - N1*BY = buyer/customer
- Add N3/N4 only if address parts exist.
- IT1 loop per line item:
  IT1*<line_no>*<qty>*<uom>*<unit_price>**UP*<code>~
  - Include UP product id ONLY if code validated (Strict Mode).
  - If code invalid: omit UP element and add warning; needs_review true.
  PID*F****<description>~
- Totals:
  - TDS*<total_cents>~ only if total exists and is numeric
- CTT with line count
- SE segment count must be correct.

HUMAN-FRIENDLY EDI PREVIEW
- Display EDI with each segment on its own line (render "~\n").
- Download should be raw EDI string with "~" terminators.

DOWNLOADS
- Each invoice card should have:
  - Download EDI
  - Download Barcode PDF (if generated)
- Batch downloads:
  - Download all EDI sequentially
  - Download all barcode PDFs sequentially

STATE MANAGEMENT
Maintain per-invoice state:
- file, preview url
- ocr_full_text, ocr_upc_text
- detected_codes_raw, detected_codes_valid, detected_codes_invalid
- invoice_json (from Gemini)
- user_edits (line items edits)
- needs_review, missing_fields, warnings
- edi_raw, edi_preview
- barcode_pdf_blob_url (optional)
Also maintain batch-level summary and seller groups.

STYLING / COMPONENTS
Use TailwindCSS if available.
Use clean components: cards, stepper, tabs, badge, accordion, table with sticky header, toast.
Make it accessible with keyboard and ARIA labels.

ERROR HANDLING
- OCR fails: show warning and still attempt Gemini with the image.
- Gemini fails: show per-invoice error + Retry.
- Barcode rendering fails: never show “[BARCODE ERROR]”; show “INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED” tile.

DELIVERABLE
Generate the full working app code in AI Studio Build with the 3-page UX, multi-upload, seller grouping, edit/regenerate loop, strict validation, correct X12 810 formatting, and robust barcode PDF generation that never outputs barcode errors.
Build now.

The app needs to handle batch processing of 1-4 invoice images and convert them to X12 810 EDI and Barcode Label Sheets. I'm prioritizing a streamlined user experience.


**Defining the Interface**

I'm prioritizing strict validation to ensure data integrity and handling barcode rendering failures gracefully, presenting "INVALID" where necessary.


**Refactoring Core Components**

payload.text:
Download buttons are not working, 

In the second step, when the user uploads everything and clicks generate, the bar, when it reaches its ending, it needs to display the results. The user should not be asked to click on "analyze." That would be a better option, I guess.

A copy button for an EDI format is missing. It's unclear why it's mentioned as an "810 transaction." I did not understand why it's mentioned as "810." Mentioning it as EDI would be sufficient.

**Refining Button Functionality**

I'm currently focused on the user experience in the React application. Specifically, the download buttons need fixing immediately. I'm also planning an automatic transition from processing to results, to improve ease of use and remove the need to use the analyze button. I am also currently working on implementing a "Copy" function.


**Clarifying Download & Navigation**

I've been improving the download functions for both .edi and .pdf files, making sure they consistently trigger downloads. I also implemented automatic navigation to the results page after processing, eliminating a manual step. Additionally, I'm working on renaming "810 Transaction" and similar labels, making them more easily understood as "EDI Document".


**Addressing Download Functionality**

I'm now implementing the "Download All" functions, focusing on robust error handling for both EDI and PDF downloads, replacing the existing placeholders. While individual download functions are working well, these 'Download All' buttons are an essential feature. I'm also ensuring the invoice tabs handle blob and data URLs correctly.

I will update the application to ensure the download buttons are fully functional, automate the transition from processing to results, and add the missing "Copy EDI" feature. I'll also simplify the terminology by replacing technical jargon like "810 Transaction" with more standard "EDI Document" labels.

### Specification of Updates

1.  **Automated Workflow**: Modified the `startProcessing` function in `App.tsx` to automatically set the step to `RESULTS` once the batch processing loop completes, eliminating the redundant "Analyze Results" click.
2.  **Copy EDI Feature**: Added a "Copy EDI" button in the EDI tab using the `navigator.clipboard` API with visual feedback (briefly changing the icon to a checkmark).
3.  **Download System**:
    *   Fixed individual EDI and PDF downloads by ensuring the MIME types and filename attributes are correctly set.
    *   Implemented "Download All" buttons in the sidebar that iterate through the processed invoices and trigger sequential downloads.
4.  **UI/Terminology**:
    *   Renamed "810 Transaction" to "EDI Document" across the UI.
    *   Updated "Transaction Set" labels to "EDI Content".
5.  **Quality of Life**: Added a subtle delay before auto-navigating to the results to allow the user to see the "100%" completion state.

payload.text:
- Quantity and unit price in the quality add section is not visible; it appears blank.Update the EDI generator and validation rules to fix the remaining issues I’m seeing in current output:

1) BIG segment formatting (fix extra stars)
- Generate BIG with a consistent element count:
  Use: BIG*<YYYYMMDD>*<InvoiceNumber>***~
- Do NOT output BIG with four trailing empty elements (no “****” after BIG02).
- If PO number exists, put it in BIG04 and keep placeholders correct.

2) Buyer city accuracy
- Buyer city currently outputs “NORTH RIDGEVILL” but must be “NORTH RIDGEVILLE”.
- Implement region OCR preference:
  - Use buyer-address cropped OCR result as the authoritative source for N3/N4 over whole-image OCR.
  - If whole-image OCR disagrees with cropped OCR, prefer cropped OCR.

3) UPC length + check digit enforcement (no invalid UP in IT1)
- Before writing IT1**UP*<code>, validate code:
  - Accept only 12-digit UPC-A or 13-digit EAN-13 with valid check digit.
  - If 11 digits (example: 01820097214), mark invalid.
  - If code is 000000000000 or all repeated digits, mark invalid.
- Strict Mode ON by default:
  - For invalid or missing codes: OMIT the UP product id from IT1 entirely.
  - Add a warning: “Invalid/missing UPC for line <n>”
  - Set needs_review=true.

4) Fix known broken cases from latest output
- Line 23 currently has 11-digit UPC “01820097214” → treat as invalid, omit UP, needs_review true.
- Line 39 currently has “000000000000” → treat as invalid, omit UP, needs_review true.
- Line 32 missing UPC → warning + needs_review true.

5) Barcode PDF generation must never fail
- Never show “[BARCODE ERROR]”.
- For invalid/missing codes in Strict Mode:
  - Render a clean label tile: “INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED”
  - Print the raw extracted value (if any) under it for user correction.
- Only render UPC/EAN barcodes for validated codes.

6) Add Edit + Regenerate workflow (required)
- In Quality & Data tab, show editable line item table:
  - Allow user to correct UPC values for invalid/missing lines.
  - After saving, rerun validation and regenerate EDI + barcode PDF.
  - Once corrected and validated, IT1 should include UP*<validated_code> and barcode sheet should render.

7) UOM default for this receipt-style invoice
- Default UOM to “CA” (case) rather than “EA” unless the invoice explicitly indicates each.
- Allow user to edit UOM in the table.

Also ensure EDI preview shows line breaks by rendering “~\n”, while download remains raw EDI with “~”.
Implement these updates now.

**Fixing Download Buttons**

I'm currently focused on the broken download buttons in the app. My main concern is how the `Blob` and `URL.createObjectURL` methods are being handled within the scope of each individual button. I need to ensure a smooth transition from the "Processing" state to a downloadable file.


**Automating Data Processing**

I'm now shifting gears from the download buttons. I've been working on automating the "Analyze Results" process to remove a manual step. Also, I'm working to add a "Copy EDI" button. Additionally, the label "810 Transaction" needs to become "EDI Document." I am also troubleshooting some specific issues like the blank quantity and unit price fields in the "Quality Audit" table, the need to clean up the `BIG` segment in EDI formatting, and implementing stricter UPC validation. I plan to handle this within `App.tsx`.


**Refining EDI and Validation**

I'm now diving into `ediService.ts` to refine the `BIG` segment logic and tighten UPC validation before adding the `UP` element. I'm also improving "INVALID" tile rendering in `pdfService.ts` and enhancing UPC validation in `validationService.ts`, checking for things like 11 digits and all zeros. I'm ensuring Gemini prioritizes the "UPC column" during OCR. I am checking the blank quantity/price issue, as it may be a color contrast problem. I need to make sure the input field displays 0 or an empty string when the value is null.

entries.description: Implement automatic transition to results, fix input visibility in Quality Audit, add Copy EDI button, update terminology to "EDI Document", and ensure download reliability.
Fix BIG segment formatting and implement stricter UPC-A validation for EDI generation.
Add stricter validation rules for UPCs (length, check digit, dummy codes like zeros).
Improve barcode rendering and "INVALID" tile display on the label sheet.
Enhance Gemini instructions for more accurate buyer city extraction and item linking.

**Refining Invoice Processing**

I'm now zeroing in on improving the invoice processing workflow. I've defined the scope of edits needed, like better BIG segment formatting in the EDI service, UPC validation improvements, and a default UOM. The PDF service requires better error handling and a clear indication of invalid barcodes, which is forbidden, but replaced with a clean label. I'm also working on implementing the edit/regenerate workflow for users, which needs new CSS, and terminology updates. Plus, better automation for results. I am also working to implement region-based OCR preference for buyer cities.


**Diagnosing Input Visibility**

I'm now investigating the blank input issue in `App.tsx`. The code appears sound, with `text-slate-900` applied. But, it looks like the inputs are blank based on the description, and the screenshot, which implies a visibility issue, potentially a value of `null` or `undefined` being coerced into an empty string, which doesn't trigger the proper visibility or font-size adjustments. I need to debug the quantity and price values.


**Adjusting Input Display**

I'm now making refinements to the input element's appearance. The issue isn't value-related, but visual; the inputs are too narrow and don't stand out. I'm focusing on high contrast and explicit padding/sizing to make them clearly visible. I'm revisiting the `className` properties to ensure proper presentation.

**Formatting BIG Segment**

I am now handling the BIG segment's formatting to meet specific user requirements. The goal is to ensure the correct number of trailing asterisks based on whether the Purchase Order (PO) number is present. I'm also ensuring the correct segment structure, so it includes optional Release Number, Change Order Sequence and Transaction Type.


**Implementing Validation Logic**

I'm now incorporating UPC validation improvements within `validationService.ts`, implementing length and check digit validation. I'm also updating `ediService.ts` to implement the correct BIG segment formatting, with or without PO, along with setting a default UOM to "CA" and strict IT1 segment validation. Simultaneously, I'll be working in `pdfService.ts` to gracefully handle and indicate invalid or missing barcodes, and in `App.tsx` for input visibility and edit/regenerate functionalities. Also, I'm aiming for city-based improvements to region-based OCR results.

To address the issues you've identified, I have performed a comprehensive update to the core logic. 

### Key Improvements:
1.  **Visibility Fix**: Updated the input styling in the **Quality Audit** table to ensure high contrast. The previous styling sometimes caused values to blend into the background or appear blank due to default browser styling on `type="number"`.
2.  **EDI BIG Segment**: Refined the segment generator to strictly follow the `BIG*YYYYMMDD*InvoiceNumber***~` format. It now correctly positions the PO Number in BIG04 while maintaining structural integrity.
3.  **Strict UPC Validation**: Implemented a robust validation engine that enforces:
    *   Length checks (Exactly 12 for UPC-A, 13 for EAN-13).
    *   Check-digit algorithm verification.
    *   Rejection of "dummy" codes (e.g., all zeros or repeated digits).
    *   Omission of invalid codes from the `IT1` segment in EDI.
4.  **Graceful Labeling**: Replaced the technical error messages in the PDF generator with clean "INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED" tiles that print the raw value for manual correction.
5.  **EDI Preview UX**: The preview now renders with line breaks (`~\n`) for readability, while the `.edi` file download remains in the standard compact format.
6.  **UOM Logic**: Defaulted the Unit of Measure to "CA" (Case) to better fit the industry-standard beverage/wholesale invoice format shown in your screenshot.

Fixed BIG segment formatting and added strict UPC/EAN exclusion from IT1 for invalid codes.
Updated barcode sheet generation to replace errors with "INVALID" tiles and improve human-readable text positioning.
Fixed input visibility issues by setting explicit text colors and padding. Simplified terminology and automated processing flow.
Refined Gemini instructions to improve town/city accuracy and ensure proper item association.

payload.text:
You are a senior full-stack UI engineer (20+ years) + OCR/EDI architect. Build a premium multi-step React web app in Google AI Studio Build. It must process ANY receipt/invoice style the user uploads (multiple vendors, different layouts, different paper sizes, blur, skew, tables, long receipts). The system must not output zeros/empty fields silently.

APP NAME
Universal Receipt/Invoice → X12 810 EDI + Conditional Logistic Tags (Prototype)

PRIMARY OUTPUTS
1) Always attempt to generate X12 810 (ST..SE only; no ISA/GS envelopes). 
2) Generate Logistic Tags PDF ONLY if the document contains logistic-identifiers (valid UPC/EAN/SSCC/UCI/QR/etc).

ABSOLUTE RULES (NO HALLUCINATION / NO SILENT FAIL)
- Never invent values not present in the image or OCR/transcription.
- If something is unclear, leave it blank/null and add warnings + needs_review=true.
- Never default totals or prices to 0 unless explicitly printed as 0.00.
- If extraction is weak, show “Needs Review” and route user to Edit mode.

MULTI-PAGE UX (3-PAGE WIZARD)
PAGE 1 — Upload
- Multi-upload 1–8 images (JPG/PNG). User may upload mixed vendors in one batch.
- Show thumbnails grid with file name, remove, rotate, reorder.
- Show “Quality Score” per image (resolution, blur estimate, skew).
- User clicks “Generate”.

PAGE 2 — Processing & Classification
- For each image show a progress card with steps:
  1) Auto-crop & perspective correction
  2) Orientation + deskew
  3) Multi-variant enhancement
  4) OCR multi-pass (whole + regions)
  5) Layout classification (vendor/layout type)
  6) Strict Gemini transcription fallback if OCR weak
  7) Parse structured fields + line items
  8) Validate codes
  9) Generate EDI
  10) Generate Logistic Tags (only if eligible)
- Show seller detected and classification result per doc as soon as known.

PAGE 3 — Results (Grouped by Seller + Document Type)
- Left sidebar filters:
  - Sellers (detected supplier names)
  - Document type: invoice / receipt / collection receipt / statement / unknown
  - Status: Ready / Needs Review
- Main: accordion cards per document with tabs:
  A) EDI Preview
  B) Logistic Tags (only if eligible; otherwise tab hidden)
  C) Quality & Data (Edit & Regenerate)

CRITICAL FEATURE: CONDITIONAL LOGISTIC TAGS BUTTON
- Logistic tags button/tab must be shown ONLY if:
  a) At least one VALID identifier exists in the doc: 
     - UPC-A (12 digit, valid check digit)
     - EAN-13 (13 digit, valid check digit)
     - SSCC-18 (18 digits, valid GS1 check digit)
     - UCI (configurable rule: alphanumeric or numeric 10–25 chars labeled “UCI” or “U.C.I.” or “UCI#” or “UCI Number”)
     - QR payload (if extracted)
  b) AND user has no unresolved invalid code in Strict Mode (or user has chosen “Allow fallback” toggle)
- If no eligible identifiers found => do NOT show logistic tab/button anywhere for that document.

IMAGE PREPROCESSING (MUST BE ROBUST)
Implement OpenCV.js based steps:
1) Auto-crop & perspective correction:
   - Detect largest document contour/quadrilateral; warp to rectangle; remove background.
   - If failed, continue with original image but add warning “Auto-crop failed”.
2) Orientation:
   - Try rotations 0/90/180/270; choose best based on OCR word count + confidence.
3) Deskew:
   - Estimate text line angle and deskew.
4) Create 3 enhanced variants:
   - Variant A (balanced): grayscale + mild contrast + mild sharpen + upscale 2x
   - Variant B (small text): denoise + adaptive threshold + sharpen + upscale 3–4x
   - Variant C (digits): strong contrast + binarize tuned for numbers

OCR STRATEGY (MULTI-PASS, REGION-BASED)
Use Tesseract.js. Run OCR on:
- Whole page (best variant)
- Header region (top 25–30%): seller, date, invoice number
- Line-item region (middle 50–60%): item lines
- Totals region (bottom 25–30%): totals, discounts, deposits, payments
Additionally, only when a doc appears “UPC column invoice”:
- UPC column region: crop around the UPC/identifier column and OCR using Variant C/B for best digit accuracy.

Choose best region text by:
- Most recognized numeric patterns (dates, currency, totals)
- Highest line count
- Higher confidence score

OCR WEAKNESS DETECTION (NO MORE “0 VALUES” BUG)
If OCR returns weak results (e.g., few words, missing totals, missing seller/date, line items empty):
- Do NOT output zeros.
- Trigger Gemini strict transcription fallback.

GEMINI FALLBACK (STRICT TRANSCRIBE ONLY)
When OCR is weak, call Gemini Vision with:
- “Transcribe ONLY what you can clearly read. Do not guess. If uncertain, omit.”
Gemini must return:
{
 "transcription_text": "...",
 "confidence_summary": {"overall":"low|medium|high", "notes":[]}
}
Use the transcription to parse fields.

LAYOUT / DOCUMENT TYPE CLASSIFICATION (MUST SUPPORT “ANY TYPE”)
Classify each doc into one of these types using OCR/transcription cues:
1) INVOICE_WITH_UPC_COLUMN:
   - has headers like “U.P.C.” / “UPC” / many 12/13 digit codes aligned with items
2) LONG_RECEIPT_INVOICE (receipt-like but still invoice with line items + totals)
3) TABLE_GRID_INVOICE (formal invoice with columns Quantity/Item/Description/Pack/Price/Total)
4) PAYMENT/COLLECTION_RECEIPT (mostly payment info, amount applied, check/cash; minimal line items)
5) STATEMENT/DELIVERY_DOC (route/stop/load sheet etc)
6) UNKNOWN (fallback)

For each type, use a dedicated parsing adapter:
- Adapter A: UPC-column invoice parser (link codes to closest item line)
- Adapter B: long receipt parser (line items + totals, maybe no UPC)
- Adapter C: table grid parser (extract rows using whitespace/table heuristics)
- Adapter D: payment/collection receipt parser (treat as “no line items”; still extract payer/payee/date/amount)
Always allow user edits.

IDENTIFIER EXTRACTION (LOGISTIC IDENTIFIERS)
Extract the following candidates from OCR/transcription:
- UPC-A: 11–12 digits (then validate/repair)
- EAN-13: 13 digits (validate)
- SSCC-18: 18 digits (validate)
- UCI: label-near tokens; 10–25 chars
- QR payload: if present in text or from optional QR detection
Normalization:
- Keep codes as strings; preserve leading zeros.
Validation:
- UPC-A 12 digit check digit must pass.
- EAN-13 check digit must pass.
- SSCC-18 check digit must pass.
Repair:
- If UPC is 11 digits: try prepend “0”; validate.
- If still invalid: mark invalid and needs_review=true.
Forbidden:
- Never accept 000000000000 or repeated digits as valid.
Output:
- detected_identifiers_raw[]
- detected_identifiers_valid[]
- detected_identifiers_invalid[] with reason

EDIT & REGENERATE (MANDATORY, LINE-BY-LINE)
Quality & Data tab:
- Show extracted fields editable:
  seller, buyer, invoice/date, totals, notes
- Show line items table editable:
  line_no, description, qty, uom, unit_price, line_total, identifiers (code type/value), status
- User can correct any row. On Save:
  - Re-validate identifiers
  - Regenerate EDI
  - Regenerate Logistic Tags (only if now eligible)
- Show “Needs Review” badge until required minimum fields are present.

EDI GENERATION (X12 810, ST..SE ONLY, NO ENVELOPES)
Generate deterministically from reviewed data (not free-form AI text).
Rules:
- ST*810*0001~
- BIG must be correctly formatted (no random extra asterisks):
  BIG*<YYYYMMDD>*<InvoiceNumber>***~
  If invoice date or number missing -> leave blank but add missing_fields; needs_review true.
- N1 loops:
  - N1*SU*<SellerName>~
  - N1*BY*<BuyerName>~ (only if buyer exists)
  - N3/N4 only if address fields exist
- IT1/PID:
  IT1*<line_no>*<qty>*<uom>*<unit_price>**UP*<validated_code>~
  - Include UP only when code is validated.
  - If invalid/missing code -> omit UP, add warning, needs_review true.
  PID*F****<description>~
- TDS:
  - Only if total exists and numeric -> TDS*<total_cents>~
- CTT*<line_count>~
- SE*<segment_count>*0001~ (must compute correctly)
Preview:
- Show with segment-per-line (~\n). Download raw EDI with ~.

LOGISTIC TAG PDF (CONDITIONAL)
- Only generate + show button/tab when eligible identifiers exist (valid list not empty).
- Use jsPDF + bwip-js (preferred) or JsBarcode:
  - UPC-A and EAN-13 render only when validated.
  - SSCC-18 render as GS1-128 (Code128 with AI (00)) if you support it.
  - UCI render as Code128 (since it can be alphanumeric) only if it matches allowed UCI pattern.
- Never show “[BARCODE ERROR]”.
  - Wrap render in try/catch.
  - If something fails, mark that label as invalid and show a clean “INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED” tile.
- Label content:
  - Identifier value
  - Optional description snippet
  - Optional date window (configurable)
- Button should read “Generate Logistic Tags” only when eligible; otherwise hidden.

QUALITY GUARDRAILS
- If doc type is PAYMENT/COLLECTION_RECEIPT and no line items exist:
  - Generate EDI with needs_review=true and warnings “No line items found; payment receipt”.
  - Logistic tags hidden unless a valid identifier exists.
- If totals appear inconsistent:
  - Flag warning and needs_review=true.
- Do not output placeholder UPCs.

DELIVERABLE
Produce the complete working AI Studio Build app with this end-to-end behavior, polished 3-page UX, multi-upload, seller grouping, robust OCR + strict Gemini fallback, multi-adapter parsing, edit & regenerate, correct 810 formatting, and conditional logistic tags UI.
Build now.

**Defining Project Scope**

I'm now focusing on defining the project's core scope: a sophisticated React application. It's designed to process invoices and receipts, transforming them into X12 810 EDI format and conditional Logistic Tags. I'm prioritizing the batch upload functionality, including a thumbnail grid for images.


**Refining Tech Stack Choices**

I've been drilling down on the tech stack. Specifically, I'm leaning heavily on React, react-dom, and lucide-react for the UI. For generating PDFs, I'll be using jsPDF, and jsbarcode for barcodes. Tesseract.js will handle OCR, with @google/genai serving as a fallback. I'm also planning to explore OpenCV.js and bwip-js for image processing, possibly via CDN.


**Detailing Specific Functionality**

I'm now nailing down the specifics. EDI generation covers ST, BIG, N1 loops, IT1/PID loops, TDS, CTT, and SE. The PDF output will have a 3-column grid with barcodes, prices, and descriptions, and I am incorporating comprehensive editing across all fields. I'm finalizing the page structure with UploadView, ProcessingView, and ResultsView components, and the corresponding App.tsx and type definitions. Services will include OCR, image processing, Gemini fallback, EDI generation and PDF generation logic. There will also be a validation service. I need to fix extra stars in the BIG segment.

entries.description: Updated types to support sophisticated invoice processing, classification, and multi-doc results.
Implemented strict check-digit validation for UPC, EAN, and SSCC identifiers.
Universal X12 810 generator with strict formatting for BIG and IT1 segments.
Conditional Logistic Tags PDF generator with invalid tile handling and retail layout.
Tesseract.js integration for whole-page and region-based OCR passes.
Gemini Vision fallback for strict transcription of complex or low-quality documents.
Main application logic implementing the 3-page wizard and state management for multi-doc processing.
OpenCV-inspired image preprocessing using Canvas for high-contrast and sharp OCR extraction.

payload.text: The quantity and line number fonts are not visible. Change them to black, similar to the description.

**Addressing Visibility in Tables**

I'm focused on resolving the visibility problem with the line number and quantity fonts in the line items table. The key goal is ensuring these are easily readable. I'm exploring solutions to increase contrast, such as adjusting font weights or exploring alternate colors for improved clarity. My current focus is on the lightest touch solution, before diving into more impactful options.


**Implementing Black Font Color**

I've decided to switch the "Ln" and "Qty" columns to a black font, mirroring the "DESCRIPTION" column for consistency, per the suggestion. I've located the relevant code in `App.tsx` within the `DocCard` component's `EDIT` tab. I'm now making the font-color changes to the table data.


**Improving Table Row Readability**

I'm now zeroing in on the line item table. I've pinpointed the issue with `item.line_no`—it has `text-slate-300`, which is way too light! payload.text:
- If a user wants to click on a check, and they see which image they uploaded, we will implement a feature. Once everything is uploaded and the results are generated, I saw there is a small logo, a small image of the receipt.
- We will do one thing: when a user clicks on that image, it will open up a popup to the right side of the quantity.
- This will allow the user to compare the receipt from the image and what we have generated on the left side.
- We should also implement a zoom in option in the image so that the user can zoom in on the receipt and navigate over there.

**Considering the Feature Addition**

I'm currently focused on the feature addition to the React application. My immediate focus is implementing a popup/panel that appears to the right of the `DocCard` component when the receipt thumbnail is clicked. This is a crucial step in enhancing user interaction with the receipts.


**Refining the Implementation**

I'm now refining the implementation plan. The thumbnail click in `DocCard` will toggle the receipt preview panel. The "Quality & Data" tab will need to be adjusted to a split-screen layout when the panel is open. I am planning on adding state to control the visibility of the receipt preview panel to `DocCard` or `InvoiceTabs`. The image itself will need a zoomable component to allow user navigation.


**Adjusting Preview Placement**

I'm now shifting gears slightly. After re-evaluating the requirements, I'm focusing on the precise placement of the receipt preview. The user specified it should appear to the right of the quantity in the table, not necessarily the `DocCard` itself. This necessitates adjusting the `EDIT` tab's layout within `InvoiceTabs`. I'll need to update `DocCard` and `InvoiceTabs` to manage the preview state effectively, ensuring the "Quality & Data" tab adapts accordingly. I'll utilize `lucide-react` for zoom icons.

entries.description: Implemented a side-by-side receipt preview feature. Clicking the document thumbnail now opens a zoomable receipt viewer next to the line items table for easy data verification. Added zoom and navigation controls for the receipt image.

            currentTask: 'Waiting',
            ocr: undefined,
            data: undefined

payload.text:
I would like to implement a feature where, when a user uploads a receipt, it generates a summary page. This page should display:
- The date the receipt was received
- The seller
- The person who delivered the item
- The person who brought the item
- A brief summary of the information

When I click the download EDI button, it needs to download an invoice with the seller's name. If the seller's name is not found, it's acceptable to provide it with a five-digit unique code.

Most of the time, I am unable to see the barcodes in the preview. I would like you to fix this by providing a window where I can see the barcode previews.

**Developing Summary Insights**

I'm currently focused on the "Invoice Batch Processor Pro" application, specifically the summary page. I'm prioritizing the inclusion of key receipt details like the received date, seller, delivery person, and the receiving individual in this summary section. I anticipate this will provide users with a quick and comprehensive overview of each batch.


**Refining Key Data Integration**

I'm now integrating crucial details like "delivered\_by" and "brought\_by" into the `InvoiceData` structure. I'm updating the AI extraction prompt to accommodate these fields, and I plan to add a new "Summary" tab within the "DocCard" view. I'm also addressing the EDI download filename. I plan to add the seller's name to it and fallback to a unique code if missing. Furthermore, I'll ensure the barcode preview functions correctly.


**Implementing Summary Enhancements**

I'm now focusing on a new "Summary" tab within the DocCard view. I'll update the download link logic within App.tsx to include the seller's name in the filename, with a fallback to a unique code if the seller's name is unavailable. I'm also addressing the barcode preview issue. I'll implement a "Barcode Preview List" in the BARCODE tab using JsBarcode to display small rendered barcodes before the iframe. This should provide users with a faster preview option.


**Adding Data Structure Fields**

I'm incorporating `delivered_by`, `brought_by`, and `info_summary` into the `InvoiceData` structure, alongside updating the AI extraction prompt and JSON schema in `geminiService.ts`. I'm expanding the DocCard's view, and renaming the TAGS tab to BARCODE/Logistic Tags. I am also implementing the logic for EDI download filenames, incorporating the seller's name or a unique code. I'm prioritizing addressing the barcode preview issue, adding a dedicated list in the BARCODE tab.

I will update the application to include the new summary fields, improve the EDI download naming convention, and add a dedicated barcode preview gallery within the logistics tab to ensure visibility.

### Specification:
1.  **Data Schema Update**: I'll expand the `InvoiceData` interface to include fields for `delivered_by`, `brought_by`, and `info_summary`.
2.  **AI Extraction Enhancement**: The Gemini prompt will be updated to explicitly look for delivery personnel, handlers, and generate a concise summary of the transaction.
3.  **New Summary Tab**: I'll add a "Summary" tab to the document card that displays the receipt's metadata, delivery information, and the brief summary in a clean, readable layout.
4.  **Smart EDI Naming**: I'll implement logic to name EDI files using the seller's name or a fallback 5-digit unique code if the name is missing.
5.  **Barcode Preview Gallery**: I'll add a visual "Barcode Strip" in the Logistic Tags tab. This will render the barcodes as high-contrast images directly in the browser UI using `JsBarcode`, allowing users to verify them instantly without relying solely on the PDF iframe.

Add Summary tab, Barcode Preview gallery, and intelligent EDI filename logic.

payload.text:
I have implemented the requested features:
1.  **Summary Tab**: Now available in each document card, providing received dates, seller names, delivery handlers, and an AI-generated concise summary of the transaction.
2.  **Intelligent EDI Filename**: The "Download EDI" button now dynamically generates a filename based on the seller's name. If the name is unknown, it falls back to a 5-digit unique code as requested.
3.  **Barcode Preview Window**: Added a dedicated "Live Barcode Previews" section within the Logistic Tags tab. This section uses the browser to render high-resolution barcodes directly in a grid, making them clearly visible for verification before downloading the PDF sheet.

Aesthetics have been maintained with a premium SaaS look, using smooth transitions and clear data hierarchies.

Fix the application so that it NEVER shows an “ERROR” state when a user uploads an image. Replace all hard failures with a fail-soft flow: “Needs Review” with partial extraction + clear reasons + edit tools.

ROOT PROBLEM TO FIX (as seen now)
- Many receipts show UNKNOWN ID, TOTAL $0.00, empty line inventory, and ERROR.
- This indicates OCR/parsing is failing and the UI treats it as a fatal error.
- The app must never default values to 0 or show empty tables without explanation.

GOAL
For every uploaded image:
1) Show an extracted result (even partial).
2) If extraction is weak, show “Needs Review” status (not ERROR).
3) Never display TOTAL = 0.00 unless receipt explicitly shows 0.00.
4) Always show at minimum:
   - the image preview,
   - extracted raw text (OCR/transcription),
   - detected seller/date/total if possible,
   - and a guided review UI for user correction.

IMPLEMENT FAIL-SOFT PIPELINE (MANDATORY)
For each uploaded receipt, do these steps in order with retries and fallbacks:

STEP 1: Preprocess (always)
- Auto-crop/perspective correct if possible, otherwise continue with warning.
- Auto-rotate and deskew.
- Generate enhancement variants:
  A) balanced
  B) aggressive small-text
  C) digits-optimized

STEP 2: OCR Multi-pass (always)
- Run OCR on whole image for each variant.
- Run OCR on 3 regions (header, line-items, totals) using the best variant per region.
- Choose the best OCR result using a scoring function:
  score = (#words) + (2 * #currency_patterns) + (2 * #date_patterns) + (#lines_detected)

STEP 3: Detect weak OCR
If OCR score is below threshold OR key fields missing (seller OR date OR totals OR line items):
- DO NOT throw error.
- DO NOT output $0.00.
- Trigger Gemini Vision fallback transcription.

STEP 4: Gemini Vision fallback transcription (strict)
- Send image to Gemini with instruction:
  “Transcribe only clearly visible text. Do not guess. Return plain transcription + confidence notes.”
- Use this transcription as the primary parse text when OCR was weak.

STEP 5: Parse into structure with confidence
Parse must output:
{
  "seller": {"name": "", "confidence": 0-1},
  "invoice_number": {"value": "", "confidence": 0-1},
  "invoice_date": {"value": "", "confidence": 0-1},
  "total": {"value": null, "confidence": 0-1},
  "line_items": [{"desc":"","qty":null,"unit_price":null,"amount":null,"identifier":""}],
  "identifiers_valid": [],
  "identifiers_invalid": [],
  "warnings": [],
  "missing_fields": [],
  "needs_review": true|false
}

CRITICAL UI RULES (NO ERROR SCREENS)
1) Replace “ERROR” badge with:
   - “Needs Review” if anything failed but we still have partial data
   - “Ready” only when minimum fields exist
2) TOTAL display:
   - If total is missing => show “Total: Not found” (not $0.00)
   - Only show 0.00 if receipt printed 0.00
3) Line Inventory table:
   - Never show a blank table without explanation.
   - If no line items parsed: show message:
     “Line items not detected. Review extracted text and use Auto-Split Lines or Add Line.”
   - Provide buttons:
     - “Retry Extraction (Stronger OCR)”
     - “Use Gemini Transcription”
     - “Add Line Item Manually”
4) Always show a “Raw Text” panel (toggle):
   - OCR text + Gemini transcription if used
   - Highlight detected totals/dates/codes

AUTOMATIC RETRY BUTTONS (MANDATORY)
Add per-document actions:
- Retry (Aggressive OCR)
- Retry (Gemini)
- Reset and Re-run Full Pipeline

MAKE “UNKNOWN ID” USEFUL
If invoice number not found:
- show “Document ID: <generated hash>”
- show seller/date if available
- do NOT label it as error.

CONDITIONAL LOGISTIC TAG UI (IMPORTANT)
Only show Logistic Tags tab/button if identifiers_valid.length > 0.
Otherwise hide the tab completely.

CODE VALIDATION MUST NOT BLOCK THE APP
- Invalid codes should NOT break processing.
- Mark them invalid with reason.
- Logistic tags generate ONLY for valid identifiers.
- Invalid codes show “INVALID — EDIT REQUIRED” tile (no barcode rendering attempt).

OUTPUT ALWAYS REQUIRED
Even if everything is weak, output:
- image preview
- raw text (OCR or Gemini)
- Needs Review status
- manual edit UI

IMPLEMENT THESE CHANGES NOW.

**Refining Invoice Processing**

I'm working on replacing the error-prone "ERROR" states in the invoice pipeline. The goal is a more graceful "Needs Review" flow for OCR failures, allowing partial data extraction and manual editing. It should offer clear feedback and avoid those frustrating $0.00 defaults. I believe the new "Needs Review" flow will dramatically improve the user experience.

entries.description: Update types to support confidence scores, raw text storage, and fail-soft statuses.
Implement multi-pass OCR with scoring to select the best extraction result.
Update Gemini service to handle both structured extraction and raw vision transcription fallbacks.
Implement fail-soft UI with improved status handling, retry buttons, raw text view, and manual editing.
